title: roasted adventurous argentinian grill yakimono
description: springy, adventurous argentinian dish featuring ginger and yakimono-grill, a perfect keto-friendly choice for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Season the flank steak with salt and black pepper.
In a large bowl, combine the sliced ginger, seeded and chopped chili pepper, minced garlic, bashed and chopped lemongrass, and thinly sliced spring onions.
Add the flank steak to the bowl and toss to coat with the ginger mixture.
Brush the yakimono-grill with olive oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the flank steak for 5-7 minutes per side, or until it reaches your desired level of doneness.
During the last 2 minutes of grilling, brush the flank steak with yuzu juice.
Transfer the grilled flank steak to a cutting board and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Slice the flank steak against the grain and serve immediately.
Slice the grilled ginger mixture and serve alongside the steak.
Garnish with chopped spring onions and serve hot.
Serve with a side of your favorite roasted vegetables, such as asparagus or Brussels sprouts.
Make sure to check the internal temperature of the steak to ensure food safety.
Make sure to let the steak rest for the recommended 5 minutes to allow the juices to redistribute.
